questions.question: Does the MarketAnalyticsResponse TypeScript type (flat, snake_case fields like market_id, total_volume, price_history) accurately match the actual backend JSON response? Other endpoints use nested wrappers (e.g. { pnl: {...}, meta: {...} }) and camelCase. If mismatched, the existing useMarketAnalytics hook silently returns wrong data.
questions.why_it_matters: If the type is wrong, the price history chart and volume chart will render with undefined data. Must verify before building components that consume this hook.

questions.question: What is the exact JSON shape of the two P&L endpoints (GET /v1/markets/{marketId}/accounts/{accountId}/pnl and GET /v1/accounts/{accountId}/pnl)? Specifically: wrapper key name ('pnl'), field casing (camelCase), and whether per-outcome data is an object keyed by outcomeId or an array.
questions.why_it_matters: Need pixel-accurate TypeScript types for the API client and React Query hooks. The backend uses camelCase for P&L fields (costBasis, unrealizedPnl) but the analytics endpoint uses snake_case (total_volume). Getting this wrong means broken deserialization.

questions.question: Where in the MarketDetail page layout should the MarketAnalytics and PnLSummary sections be placed? The plan says 'collapsible section' for analytics and 'alongside the analytics section' for P&L. Should they go between ProbabilityBar and the trading panels, or after the trading section before the Event Journal?
questions.why_it_matters: MarketDetail already has 8+ sections. Placing analytics/PnL in the wrong spot disrupts the user flow — analytics data should be visible before trading decisions, not buried below.

questions.question: Should PnLSummary gracefully hide when the account has never traded in a market (backend returns 404), or show a 'No trades yet' placeholder? Similarly, should it render at all when no accountId is configured?
questions.why_it_matters: The P&L endpoint returns 404 for 'never traded' vs 200 with zero values for 'traded but net-zero'. The component needs distinct handling for: no account configured, never traded (404), and zero P&L (200).

questions.question: Should the price history LineChart show one line per outcome (multi-line chart from marginals object) or a single aggregated line? The analytics endpoint returns marginals: Record<string, number> per timestamp with all outcomes.
questions.why_it_matters: Multi-line chart requires dynamic color assignment per outcome and a legend. Single-line requires choosing which outcome to show. This significantly affects the recharts configuration and component complexity.

questions.question: What polling interval should the new P&L hooks use? The parent plan says '10-30s' for analytics polling. Existing hooks vary: useMarkets uses 5s, useMarket uses 5s, useMarketAnalytics has no polling. Should useMarketPnl and useAccountPnl poll, and at what rate?
questions.why_it_matters: P&L data changes only when trades execute. Aggressive polling wastes bandwidth; no polling means stale data after trades. The right interval affects perceived responsiveness of the analytics dashboard.

questions.question: How should recharts charts be themed to match the existing dark-themed inline-style UI? The codebase uses CSS variables (--color-primary, --color-success, etc.) but recharts accepts hex/rgb strings as props, not CSS variable references.
questions.why_it_matters: If charts use hardcoded colors that don't match the CSS variable palette, the analytics section will look visually disconnected from the rest of the app.

questions.question: Are there existing frontend tests (vitest + React Testing Library) for the hooks and route components that I should follow as patterns? If so, should new analytics components have test coverage, or is that deferred to step 9 of the plan?
questions.why_it_matters: Step 9 of the parent plan covers 'integration tests' but doesn't specify frontend vs backend. If frontend component tests are expected, they need to be written alongside the components, not bolted on later.

assumptions.assumption: recharts is the correct charting library as specified in the parent plan. It will be installed as a production dependency via npm.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to use a different library (d3, chart.js, visx) which have very different APIs and integration patterns.

assumptions.assumption: New components should use inline React.CSSProperties with CSS variables, matching the existing codebase pattern. No CSS modules, Tailwind, or styled-components.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to adopt a different styling approach, potentially requiring build config changes.

assumptions.assumption: New feature components go in frontend/src/features/analytics/ directory, following the existing domain-based organization (features/market/, features/trading/, features/graph/).
assumptions.if_wrong: Components might belong in features/market/ or features/trading/ if analytics is not considered a separate domain.

assumptions.assumption: The existing useMarketAnalytics hook and getMarketAnalytics client function are correctly wired and working. No changes needed to these — only new P&L hooks/client functions are required.
assumptions.if_wrong: May need to fix the analytics hook before building the MarketAnalytics chart component, adding unexpected scope.

assumptions.assumption: No new routes are needed. MarketAnalytics and PnLSummary embed in MarketDetail; AccountPnL embeds in Portfolio. This is explicitly stated in the parent plan.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need router changes and new page components, significantly expanding scope.

assumptions.assumption: The P&L backend endpoints (merged in ff/Tuy1-execute-uy1-backend-pnl) are available on the current branch and return the documented response shapes with camelCase keys and { pnl: {...}, meta: {...} } wrapper.
assumptions.if_wrong: Types would be wrong and hooks would fail. Would need to read the actual backend handler code to verify.

assumptions.assumption: The price history chart should show multi-line (one line per outcome) since the analytics endpoint returns marginals with all outcome probabilities per timestamp.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need a simpler single-outcome chart or an outcome selector dropdown, changing the component design.

assumptions.assumption: The interval selector for price history (1h, 6h, 1d, 7d) should be a simple button group using the existing inline-style pattern, with useState to track selection.
assumptions.if_wrong: Might need a dropdown or more complex selector pattern if the design calls for it.

assumptions.assumption: Frontend component tests are deferred to step 9 of the plan and are out of scope for this task (steps 4-8 and 10). Step 10 is end-to-end verification, not unit tests.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to write vitest + RTL tests for each new component alongside implementation, adding significant scope.

assumptions.assumption: P&L hooks should use a 30s refetchInterval — frequent enough for post-trade updates but not wasteful. Analytics hook (useMarketAnalytics) should add a 30s refetchInterval too.
assumptions.if_wrong: Different polling rates could cause data inconsistency between analytics and P&L displays, or unnecessarily hammer the backend.
